description_long: This is a two-story house with a flat, sand-colored exterior that resembles adobe or stucco construction, typical of Middle Eastern or North African urban environments. The building features a flat roof with a fabric canopy supported by four poles, providing shade to a portion of the rooftop. The windows are small, rectangular, and protected by metal grilles, arranged in symmetrical rows on both floors. The front facade includes a recessed entryway with two supporting columns and a small overhang, as well as a set of steps leading up to the main door. The overall structure is boxy with slightly protruding architectural details around the windows and corners, and the surface texture is rough and weathered, suggesting durability in arid climates.
description: A two-story, sand-colored house with a flat roof, rooftop canopy, and symmetrical grilled windows. The facade features a recessed entryway with columns and a small overhang.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway with columns, steps, and two grilled windows flanking the door; rooftop canopy is visible.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the symmetrical arrangement of grilled windows on both floors and the rooftop canopy extending over part of the roof.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the flat roof, rooftop canopy, and a row of grilled windows; no visible entryway.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with symmetrical grilled windows and the rooftop canopy; the entryway columns are partially visible.
attribute_entryway_design: Recessed entry with steps, two supporting columns, and a small overhang
